etymology.description: semantic 言(言) (yán, "word; speech") + phonetic 射(射) (shè) -> express words of thanks or apologies
etymology.interpretation: The character combines 言(言) (yán, "word; speech") to indicate the act of speaking with 射(射) (shè) to provide the sound, forming the basis for meanings related to speaking words of gratitude or apology. The core sense relates to expressing thanks through speech, while 'apologize' involves acknowledging fault through words, and 'wither' is a figurative extension reflecting fading or diminishing, similar to the waning impact of words over time.
etymology.simplification: Simplified to 谢 by replacing 言(言) (yán, "word; speech") with its simplified version 讠.

examples.chinese: 谢谢你的帮助(謝謝你的幫助)。
他道了歉(他道了歉)，大家都很满意(大家都很滿意)。
examples.pinyin: Tā dàole qiàn, dàjiā dōu hěn mǎnyì.
examples.english: He apologized, and everyone was satisfied.

examples.chinese: 秋风一吹(秋風一吹)，树叶渐渐枯萎(樹葉漸漸枯萎)。
examples.pinyin: Qiūfēng yī chuī, shùyè jiànjiàn kūwěi.
examples.english: When the autumn wind blows, the leaves gradually wither.
